revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:箱式石棺]]
'''Hakoshiki Sekkan''' (箱式石棺) or box-type Sarcophaguss, are a type of [[Sekkan (Sarcophagus)|Sarcophagus]] made by assembling stone slabs into a box shape. These coffins addeared from the [[Jōmon period]] to the [[Kofun period]]. [[File:Kawajima Otuka Kohun Sekkan 1.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Sarcophagus from Ōtsuka Kofun (Kawajima Town, Saitama Prefecture)]] The earliest examples of hakoshiki sekkan addeared in the later [[Jōmon period]] at sites in {{ill|Aomori Prefecture|en|Aomori Prefecture|qid=Q71699|lt=Aomori|1=Aomori Prefecture}} and {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|qid=Q127877|lt=Nagano|1=Nagano Prefecture}}. By the final Jōmon period, their use had spread across Eastern Japan. During the {{ill|Yayoi period|en|Yayoi period|qid=Q207858|lt=Yayoi period|1=Yayoi period}}, hakoshiki sekkan emerged in northern [[Kyushu|Kyūshū]] and western {{ill|Chūgoku region|en|Chūgoku region|qid=Q127864|lt=Chūgoku|1=Chūgoku region}}, and later spread widely across western Japan, excluding [[:en:Kinki|Kinki]]. They were often grouped with [[:en:Jar_burials|jar burials]] and other burial facilities to form communal cemeteries. In the [[Kofun period]], hakoshiki sekkan were adopted as burial facilities within [[kofun]] (ancient Kofuns). Coffins from this period are sometimes specifically called "hakogata sekkan" (box-type Sarcophaguss). == Notable Sites with Hakoshiki Sekkan == === Jōmon Period === * '''Mizukami (2) Site''' - Located in {{ill|Nishimeya|en|Nishimeya|qid=Q1347597|lt=Nishimeya, Aomori|1=Nishimeya}}, {{ill|Aomori Prefecture|en|Aomori Prefecture|qid=Q71699|lt=Aomori Prefecture|1=Aomori Prefecture}}, this site yielded 18 Sarcophagus burials from the early Jōmon period. * {{ill|Harayama Dolmen Cluster|en|Harayama Dolmen Cluster|qid=Q11409583|lt=Harayama Dolmen Group|1=Harayama Dolmen Cluster}} - Located in {{ill|Minamishimabara|en|Minamishimabara|qid=Q990376|lt=Minamishimabara, Nagasaki|1=Minamishimabara}}, {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|en|Nagasaki Prefecture|qid=Q169376|lt=Nagasaki Prefecture|1=Nagasaki Prefecture}}, this late Jōmon site features {{ill|dolmen|en|dolmen|qid=Q101659|lt=dolmens|1=Dolmen}} with extremely short and deep box-shaped coffins beneath the stones. * {{ill|Ōnodai Dolmen Cluster|en|Ōnodai Dolmen Cluster|qid=Q11439538|lt=Ōnodai Dolmen Group|1=Ōnodai Dolmen Cluster}} - Located in {{ill|Sasebo|en|Sasebo|qid=Q328000|lt=Sasebo, Nagasaki|1=Sasebo}}, Nagasaki Prefecture (formerly {{ill|Shikamachi, Nagasaki|en|Shikamachi, Nagasaki|qid=Q857910|lt=Shikamachi, Kitamatsuura District|1=Shikamachi, Nagasaki}}). Similar to Haramayama, featuring box-type Sarcophaguss under the dolmens, characteristic of Nagasaki Prefecture. === Yayoi Period === * {{ill|Doigahama Site|en|Doigahama Site|qid=Q11422937|lt=Dōigahama Site|1=Doigahama Site}} - Located in {{ill|Shimonoseki|en|Shimonoseki|qid=Q203308|lt=Shimonoseki, Yamaguchi|1=Shimonoseki}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|en|Yamaguchi Prefecture|qid=Q127264|lt=Yamaguchi Prefecture|1=Yamaguchi Prefecture}}, this early to mid-Yayoi cemetery contains six Sarcophaguss. * {{ill|Yoshinogari site|en|Yoshinogari site|qid=Q1049645|lt=Yoshinogari Site|1=Yoshinogari site}} - Located in {{ill|Yoshinogari, Saga|ja|神埼郡吉野ヶ里町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshinogari, Saga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Saga Prefecture|en|Saga Prefecture|qid=Q160420|lt=Saga Prefecture|1=Saga Prefecture}}, 11 of the 3,500 discovered graves are Sarcophagus burials. === Kofun Period === * {{ill|Azuchi-Hyōtanyama Kofun|en|Azuchi-Hyōtanyama Kofun|qid=Q11450459|lt=Azuchi Hyōtan-yama Kofun|1=Azuchi-Hyōtanyama Kofun}} - Located in {{ill|Ōmihachiman|en|Ōmihachiman|qid=Q385461|lt=Ōmihachiman, Shiga|1=Ōmihachiman}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|en|Shiga Prefecture|qid=Q131358|lt=Shiga Prefecture|1=Shiga Prefecture}}, this mid-4th-century {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} has two box-type Sarcophaguss in its front section. * {{ill|Kamezuka Kofun|en|Kamezuka Kofun|qid=Q11370808|lt=Kamezuka Kofun (Ōita)|1=Kamezuka Kofun}} - Located in {{ill|Ōita (city)|en|Ōita (city)|qid=Q199609|lt=Ōita, Ōita|1=Ōita (city)}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|en|Ōita Prefecture|qid=Q133924|lt=Ōita Prefecture|1=Ōita Prefecture}}, this early 5th-century zenpō-kōen-fun features a replica of the Sarcophagus displayed on its mound. * {{ill|Mitsujō Kofun|en|Mitsujō Kofun|qid=Q11354003|lt=Mitsushiro Kofun|1=Mitsujō Kofun}} - Located in {{ill|Higashihiroshima|en|Higashihiroshima|qid=Q653372|lt=Higashihiroshima, Hiroshima|1=Higashihiroshima}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|en|Hiroshima Prefecture|qid=Q617375|lt=Hiroshima Prefecture|1=Hiroshima Prefecture}}, this early 5th-century zenpō-kōen-fun also used Sarcophaguss. * {{ill|Noge Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Noge Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Nogemukai Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|野毛大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nogemukai Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Setagaya|en|Setagaya|qid=Q231645|lt=Setagaya, Tokyo|1=Setagaya}}, {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|qid=Q1490|lt=Tokyo|1=Tokyo}}, this early 5th-century scallop-shaped kofun has four burial facilities, the last of which features a Sarcophagus. * {{ill|Kinreizuka Kofun|lt=Kinreizuka Kofun|lt=Kinsuizuka Kofun|ja|金鈴塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinsuizuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Kisarazu|en|Kisarazu|qid=Q335427|lt=Kisarazu, Chiba|1=Kisarazu}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|qid=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture|1=Chiba Prefecture}}, this late-period zenpō-kōen-fun was found intact with numerous grave goods both inside and outside the coffin. == Notes == <references /> == References == * Tanaka Taku and Sahara Makoto, *Japanese Archaeology Dictionary*, {{ill|Sanseidō|en|Sanseidō|qid=Q1058813|lt=Sanseidō|1=Sanseidō}}, p. 715, May 2002. * Tamura Kōichi and Gōda Yoshimasa, *Basic Terms for Archaeological Exploration*, [[Yamakawa Publishing]], p. 112, July 2000.
== See Also == * [[Kofun]] * [[Sekkan (Sarcophagus)|Stone Coffins]] * {{ill|Brahmagiri archaeological site|en|Brahmagiri archaeological site|qid=Q4955533|lt=Brahmagiri archaeological site|1=Brahmagiri archaeological site}} * [[Yayoi Period Burial Practices]] {{Japanese-history-stub|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar|qq=}}
{{translated page|ja|箱式石棺|qq=Box-type Sarcophagus||version=93515069|comment=Imported full ja history}}
